name: python-decouple (Settings Separation from Code)

agent_readiness.notes: 12-factor app configuration from .env or .ini files. from decouple import config. DEBUG = config('DEBUG', default=False, cast=bool). DATABASE_URL = config('DATABASE_URL'). MAX_CONNECTIONS = config('MAX_CONNECTIONS', default=100, cast=int). config('ALLOWED_HOSTS', cast=Csv()) → ['host1', 'host2'] from comma-separated string. .env file: DEBUG=True, DATABASE_URL=postgres://..., or settings.ini: [settings] DEBUG=True. Priority: environment variables > .env > settings.ini > default. UndefinedValueError if no value and no default. Csv() for list parsing. config.search_path for custom .env location. AutoConfig for path discovery. Lighter than python-dotenv+pydantic-settings combination for simple cases.

gotchas: No type annotations — limited IDE support
Less powerful than pydantic-settings for complex config validation
AutoConfig path discovery can be confusing in non-standard project structures
No nested config support (flat key=value only)
best_when: Simple Django/Flask projects wanting 12-factor app config
When python-dotenv is too bare and pydantic-settings is too heavy
Quick configuration with type casting and defaults
avoid_when: Complex config with validation, nesting, or schemas (use pydantic-settings)
When dynaconf's multi-environment features are needed
summary: python-decouple separates settings from code. config('DEBUG', default=False, cast=bool); config('HOSTS', cast=Csv()). Priority: env vars > .env > settings.ini > default. UndefinedValueError if missing. MIT. No type annotations. Simpler than pydantic-settings for basic use. Best for Django/Flask 12-factor config. Use pydantic-settings for validation/schemas or dynaconf for multi-environment configs.
